variants.title: EEE-Bench 开放题
variants.rewritten_question_text:
Question: Let \( x(t) \) represent a periodic signal with period \( T = 8 \) seconds, whose Fourier series coefficients are
\[
a_k = \begin{cases} 
\frac{1}{j \pi k} & \text{for integers } k \neq 0 \\
0 & k = 0
\end{cases}
\]
When \( x(t) \) is the input to a linear, time-invariant system with system function \( H(s) \) is shown in (a),
the output \( y(t) \) is the sum of a constant \( \beta \) plus a periodic train of impulses with area 1,
\[
y(t) = \beta + \sum_{k=-\infty}^{\infty} \delta(t - 8k)
\]
as shown in the (b).
Consider the output of the same system if the period of the input signal is changed to T = 4 seconds while keeping the Fourier series coefficients ak
unchanged.
Is it possible to determine the new output signal from the information provided?

rationale: Converted multiple choice into blank-style open-ended question.

variants.title: EEE-Bench 子题 1
variants.rewritten_question_text:
Question: The state space equation of a system is described by 
\[
\dot{x} = Ax + Bu, \quad y = Cx
\]
where \( x \) is the state vector, \( u \) is the input, \( y \) is the output, and 
\[
A = \begin{bmatrix} 0 & 1 \\ 0 & -2 \end{bmatrix}, \quad B = \begin{bmatrix} 0 \\ 1 \end{bmatrix}, \quad C = [1 \quad 0]
\]

A unity feedback is provided to the above system \( G(s) \) to make it a closed-loop system as shown in the . 

For a unit step input \( r(t) \), the steady-state error in the input will be ______.
请只回答第 1 个目标量。
